title: rosa × andegavensis var. transmota
intro: Rosa × andegavensis var. transmota, also known as the alpine rose or Andegavensis rose, is a species of rose that is native to the European Alps. It is a hybrid of Rosa pendulina and Rosa glauca, characterized by its delicate white or pale pink flowers and glossy green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The alpine rose can be prepared as a herbal remedy by infusing its flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using its extracts in ointments and creams to promote healthy skin and reduce inflammation.
